math_seq_1: A train leaves Station A at 9:00 traveling east at 60 mph. Another train leaves Station B (240 miles east of A) at 10:00 traveling west at 40 mph. At what time will they meet?
If both trains had instead departed at 9:00, when would they meet?
Using your previous answer, how far from Station A would the meeting point be?
If the eastbound train made a 20-minute stop at 9:30, how would the meeting time change?
Summarize the three scenarios above in one sentence each.
logic_box_1: There are two boxes. Box 1 is either full or empty. Box 2 is either full or empty. If Box 1 is full, then Box 2 is empty. If Box 2 is full, then Box 1 is empty. I checked Box 1 and it was full. What can you infer about Box 2?
Now I add a Box 3. Box 3 is full only if Box 2 is empty. Is Box 3 full or empty?
What if a new rule is added: at most one box can be full at a time. Does this contradict any of your earlier conclusions?
Given the rule from the previous turn, list every consistent assignment of (Box 1, Box 2, Box 3).
Out of the assignments you just listed, which one matches the original premise that Box 1 is full?
negotiation_1: I am selling a road bike. Asking $400. You are a buyer. What is your first offer?
I accept $325 if you also take the helmet. Do we have a deal?
I changed my mind on the helmet. Does your $325 still stand?
If I throw in the bike lock instead of the helmet, what would you offer now?
Summarize the final terms of our deal.
coin_chain_1: A coin is heads up. Alex flips it. Is it still heads up?
Then Bob flips it. Is it still heads up?
Then Carol does not flip it. Is it still heads up?
Finally, Dan flips it twice in a row. Which side is up now?
List the side of the coin after each step in order.
